summary.intent: Implement tasks #297, #298, and #299: build 8 agnostic color UI components (ColorSwatch, ColorField, ColorArea, ColorSlider, ColorWheel, AngleSlider, ColorSwatchPicker) with a shared ColorValue foundation in ars-core, reconcile specs, and ship as a single PR.
summary.outcome: All 8 components implemented, fully tested (98 component tests + 9 proptests), clippy-clean in std and no_std, spec files reconciled, cargo xci-fast passing, committed and pushed to GitHub.

repo: ars-core is built --no-default-features (no_std) in CI via feature_matrix.rs:56; transcendental float methods (powf, cbrt, atan2) must go through core_maths::CoreFloat using fully-qualified call syntax, matching the convention already used in ars-i18n.
core_maths::CoreFloat does not provide min/max; bare-metal equivalents must be hand-written as fmin/fmax helpers in no_std code.
MessageFn only has From impls for a fixed set of fn signatures; new color-specific signatures (e.g. Fn(&ColorNameParts, &Locale)) require new From<F> impls added to crates/ars-core/src/message_fn.rs.

#[derive(ComponentPart)] fails for structs containing f64 fields (not Eq/Hash); AngleSlider's Marker{value: f64} requires a manual ComponentPart impl, following the same pattern already used in ars-components/src/input/slider/mod.rs.
CssProperty::Custom renders as --{name}, so the name argument must NOT include the -- prefix; spec had it doubled.
cargo xci-fast enforces snapshot_count == state_variant_count per component; missing a snapshot for any state is a gate failure.
The workspace denies clippy::suspicious, which means any new stable Rust release surfacing a lint in that group becomes a CI blocker for the whole repo — not just modified files.
Spec code blocks tagged ```rust are compiled in isolation by the spec-compile-snippets gate; cross-block symbol references require ```rust,no_check to avoid false failures.
Callback type is Callback<dyn Fn(T)+Send+Sync>, not Callback<T> — the spec used the shorthand form throughout.

Drag components (ColorArea, ColorSlider, ColorWheel, AngleSlider) follow the slider pattern: the machine handles Idle↔Dragging transitions and value math; the adapter wires pointer events; on_change_end fires via a typed Effect enum on DragEnd. The spec's use_platform_effects()/track_pointer_drag() calls do not exist in the codebase.
proptest-regressions/ files in ars-components must be committed alongside new proptests; a new regression seed written there is a legitimate shrunk failure case to preserve.
ars-components has a hard 90.5% line coverage threshold enforced by cargo xci-fast; initial stateful component implementations at 70-82% require dense per-module coverage tests.

code.finding: Add new From<F> impls here for each novel fn signature used by color component Messages structs; no generic macro — each signature is a hand-written impl block.

Pre-existing clippy::unnecessary_option_map_or_else (map_or_else(.., identity) → unwrap_or_else) that was not surfaced until stable 1.96 shipped. Fixed in this PR as an unrelated 1-line unblock since the workspace denies clippy::suspicious.

connect() proptest assertions must be behavioral (e.g. parse aria-valuenow and assert it tracks machine value), not bare let _attrs = api.thumb_attrs() discard — the latter only smoke-tests no-panic and is flagged by let_underscore_drop.

New flat module (not color_picker.rs) re-exported at ars_core crate root. Contains ColorValue, ColorChannel, ColorSpace, ColorFormat, ColorNameParts, DragTarget, all conversions, WCAG contrast, OKLCH perceptual naming, parse/format helpers. Requires core_maths dep.
AngleSlider needs three snapshot tests (Idle-root, Dragging-root, Focused-thumb) to satisfy cargo xci-fast's state_variant_count==snapshot_count gate; initial implementation only had two.

workflow: Cargo.toml edits require explicit user approval per CLAUDE.md before proceeding; always ask before adding a new workspace dependency.
User preference: avoid single-letter variable names even in math-heavy color conversion code — use hue_sextant, chroma, linear_red, etc. instead of h, c, r.
cargo xci-fast is the mandatory pre-push gate (~5 min); running it before committing catches snapshot count violations and coverage regressions that unit tests alone won't surface.

Spec reconciliation is part of the implementation task: after building against a spec, update the spec's code blocks to match the real APIs (Callback types, Effect enums, import paths, removed nonexistent helpers) so the snippet-compile gate stays green.

When agent exploratory reports conflict (e.g. on where ColorValue lives), verify directly with Read/Grep before trusting the summary — paraphrase drift on arity, import paths, and type spellings is common.
Proptest _attrs discard bindings exist only to dodge let_underscore_drop on Drop types, not as real assertions; always convert them to connect↔state invariant checks.

friction: core_maths::CoreFloat missing min/max caused a build failure after the initial no_std conversion; required discovering fmin/fmax workaround by grepping CoreFloat's actual method list.
Spec's use_platform_effects() and track_pointer_drag() patterns don't exist in the codebase, requiring a full reconciliation of the drag machine pattern across 4 component specs.
MessageFn::new coercion doesn't work for novel fn signatures — required finding and extending the From<F> pattern, which isn't documented and only discoverable by reading message_fn.rs.
color_name_parts() arity was inconsistent between the swatch spec (no-arg) and the picker spec (locale, messages arg); required judgment call to resolve.
KeyboardEventData field names in the implementation differed from the test helper assumptions, causing a compile failure that required re-reading the struct definition.
cargo xci-fast's snapshot count gate caught a missing AngleSlider Dragging state snapshot only after a full ~5 min CI run, not during unit test runs.
Pre-existing pagination clippy lint on stable 1.96 would have blocked CI for the entire repo — unrelated to the task but required a fix before pushing.
The type inference for context_only closures throughout color_field cascaded through ~10 sites, requiring annotating every |ctx: &mut Context<M>| closure param individually.
Initial component coverage was 70-82%, well below the 90.5% threshold; required a second pass of dense coverage tests across all 7 stateful modules.
open_items: Adapter specs (crates/leptos-components/specialized/color-*.md and dioxus equivalent) describe framework-specific adapter components for all 8 new components — explicitly out of scope per the issues, deferred to a future adapter task.

ColorValue and its associated types could be promoted from spec/components/specialized/color-picker.md §Types to a dedicated spec/shared/color-types.md; deferred because it touches manifest.toml and 8 spec frontmatters without affecting implementation correctness.
transitive dependency block v0.1.6 has a future-incompat warning (pre-existing, not introduced here); needs upstream fix or dep upgrade outside this PR's scope.
Insta snapshot tests for i18n-gated highlight component only run under the i18n feature flag; they show as unreferenced in the fast tier but are covered by the full xci feature matrix run.
